conflicts_of_interest_inventory.description: Co-owns Extended Family Preschool with wife Jessica Young Haney. While a preschool is not a direct competitor or vendor to the ISD, it exists within the broader education ecosystem feeding into Hallsville ISD. Low conflict concern.

about_public_record.about_summary_narrative:
Dale E. Haney is the Vice President of the Hallsville ISD Board, serving Place 7 since 2020. He is a 1993 Hallsville High School graduate and Marine veteran. Haney worked approximately 20 years as a power plant operator at AEP's Pirkey plant and currently works at AEP's J. Lamar Stall Unit in Shreveport.

He co-owns Extended Family Preschool with his wife, Jessica Young Haney (1999 HHS Salutatorian, UT Tyler graduate, former LISD teacher). He won the Place 7 seat in 2020 over John Craig and filed for reelection in February 2026.

The preschool co-ownership is noted as a low-severity conflict: while not a direct vendor or competitor, it places the family within the education ecosystem. No negative public-record items have surfaced as of this research date.

summary: Dale E. Haney is the Vice President of the Hallsville ISD Board (Place 7). 1993 HHS graduate. Marine veteran. AEP power plant operator (20 years at Pirkey, now J. Lamar Stall Unit in Shreveport). Co-owns Extended Family Preschool with wife Jessica Young Haney. Won 2020 over John Craig. Filed for reelection Feb 2026. Clean criminal record in open-source review.
analyst_notes: Haney is a long-rooted Hallsville family (1993 HHS grad, wife 1999 HHS Salutatorian). The preschool co-ownership is the only notable intersection with the education space. Marine veteran status and 20+ years at AEP suggest a stable, blue-collar profile. His 2020 win over John Craig and subsequent rise to VP suggest board establishment support. Priority next moves: locate social media, pull Harrison CAD property records, pull voter roll primary history, check Extended Family Preschool licensing and any DHS complaints, pull meeting minutes for attendance and vote patterns.
